title: Bad Hair Day
description: Test your understanding of the video and practise useful barbershop vocabulary, haircut expressions and action verbs.

questions.question: What is a “barbershop”?
options: A place where you get your hair cut
A place where you buy clothes
A place where you wait for a flight
questions.answer: A place where you get your hair cut
questions.explanation: A barbershop is a place where people go to get their hair cut.

questions.question: What is a “cape” at the barbershop?
options: A large cloth put over you to keep hair off your clothes
A small mirror used to see your teeth
A tool used to cut hair very short
A bottle used to make hair wet
questions.answer: A large cloth put over you to keep hair off your clothes
questions.explanation: The hairdresser drapes a cape over Sune's shoulders to keep hair off his clothes.

questions.question: What does “drape” mean?
options: To place cloth loosely over something
questions.answer: To place cloth loosely over something
questions.explanation: The hairdresser drapes the cape over Sune's shoulders.

questions.question: What does “dampen” mean?
questions.explanation: The hairdresser dampens Sune's hair with a spray bottle.

questions.question: What is a “snip”?
options: A small quick cut with scissors
questions.answer: A small quick cut with scissors
questions.explanation: A snip is a small, quick cut made with scissors.

questions.question: What are “clippers”?
options: An electric tool used to cut hair very short
A cloth worn around the neck
questions.answer: An electric tool used to cut hair very short
questions.explanation: Clippers are used to cut hair very short, usually on the sides.

questions.question: What does “unconvincing” mean?
questions.explanation: Sune gives an unconvincing smile because it does not look real or believable.

questions.question: What does “grow out” mean?
options: To let your hair get longer until it goes back to normal
To cut your hair shorter and shorter
To wash your hair very quickly
To hide your hair under a cape
questions.answer: To let your hair get longer until it goes back to normal
questions.explanation: Some bad haircuts need to grow out before they look normal again.

questions.question: What does “flatten” mean?
options: To make something flat or smooth
questions.answer: To make something flat or smooth
questions.explanation: Outside, Sune tries to flatten his hair with one hand.

sections.title: Video comprehension
sections.instructions: Answer the questions about the story.

questions.question: Where does Sune go at the beginning of the video?
questions.explanation: Sune walks into the barbershop to get his hair cut.

questions.question: What does the hairdresser put over Sune's shoulders?
questions.explanation: The hairdresser drapes a cape over Sune's shoulders.

questions.question: What does Sune point at in the magazine?
questions.explanation: Sune points confidently at a photo of a sharp, stylish cut.

questions.question: What happens after the first snip?
options: A chunk of fur falls to the floor
questions.answer: A chunk of fur falls to the floor
questions.explanation: The first snip sends a chunk of fur to the floor.

questions.question: What does Sune do when the hairdresser shows him the back?
options: He asks for a different chair
questions.explanation: Sune has no idea what to say, so he nods slowly.

sections.title: Complete the sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or expression.

questions.question: The hairdresser used a spray bottle to ______ Sune's hair.
questions.explanation: To dampen means to make something slightly wet.

questions.question: The hairdresser used ______ to cut Sune's hair very short on the sides.
questions.explanation: Clippers are the electric tool used to cut hair very short.

questions.question: Sune gave a big, wide, completely ______ smile.
questions.explanation: Unconvincing means something does not look real or believable.
